hook.text: One Ebola contact tracer in Nigeria tracked 894 contacts. All were monitored. Zero spread. Outbreak contained.

buildup.text: Contact tracing identifies everyone an infected person interacted with during their contagious period.

discovery.text: Each contact is tested, monitored, and isolated if positive—breaking the chain of transmission.

twist.text: Contact tracing works brilliantly for diseases with clear symptoms but struggles with asymptomatic spread.

climax.text: Digital tools and rapid testing have accelerated tracing but can't replace human investigators.

punchline.text: Find every contact. Test. Isolate. Repeat. Outbreaks die at broken chains.

quiz.question: Why is contact tracing less effective against diseases with asymptomatic spread?
options: Asymptomatic carriers don't know they're infected so they can't be traced from symptoms
Contact tracers refuse to work with those cases
